title: Animal Food/Supplies Donation Programs - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Volunteering & Donating > Animal Food/Supplies Donation Programs 23 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Disaster/Emergency Issues Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness GLIDE REVITALIZATION DONATIONS PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accepts small donations of shelf-stable food, pet food, clothing, small appliances, compact furniture, and household goods. Cannot accept large appliances or big furniture. Supports individuals and families impacted by the March 2025 floods in Douglas County. 301 Glide Loop Drive Glide, OR 97443 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-4pm Email (541) 671-0189 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BROOKINGS CORE RESPONSE PEER HOUSE NAVIGATION CENTER PEER HOUSE DONATIONS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accepts donations of clothing, personal grooming supplies, and pet food. Does not accept open or used personal grooming supplies. 16389 Highway 101 S Brookings, OR 97415 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-3pm Email (541) 251-0825 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CASCADE AIDS PROJECT ESTHER'S PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a shopping-style food pantry, pet food, and personal care items that are available once a month. People without an HIV diagnosis may be eligible to receive a one-time food box of pantry staple items. Accepts donations of shelf-stable food, pet food, toilet paper, and personal care items. 10202 SE 32nd Avenue Suite 601 Milwaukie, OR 97222 Eligibility: Individuals diagnosed with HIV who have a monthly income below 185% of the Federal Poverty Level Hours: Monday 1pm-4pm, Tuesday/Thursday 2pm-5pm, Wednesday 1pm-3pm Email (503) 349-4699 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DALLAS FOOD BANK FOOD PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a shopping-style food pantry with a 3 to 5 day supply of food. Individuals may visit the pantry once per month and are encouraged to bring their own bags or boxes for transporting food items. Also has pet food. May be able to provide toilet paper, dish soap, laundry soap, and bath soap on a limited basis. Accepts donations of food and pet food. 322 Main Street Suite 180 Dallas, OR 97338 Eligibility: Must meet USDA income guidelines Hours: Pantry: Monday-Tuesday/Thursday 1pm-3pm, Wednesday/Friday 9am-11am Food Donations: Monday-Friday 9am-11am Email (503) 623-3578 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ESTACADA AREA FOOD BANK Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Shopping-style food pantry provides a 5-day supply of food to households. Also offers pet food, hygiene items, household supplies, and clothing as available. 272 S Broadway Street Estacada, OR 97023 Eligibility: USDA-TEFAP income guidelines. SNAP, TANF, SSI, LIHEAP recipients automatically eligible. Hours: Pantry: 2nd and 4th Tuesdays of the month 10am-2pm, Wednesday 2pm-6pm, Thursday 10am-2pm Donations: Tuesday/Thursday 10am-2pm, Wednesday 2pm-6pm Email (503) 630-2888 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FIDO PET FOOD BANK Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides pet food for cats and dogs. Donations of pet food and supplies are accepted on-site and at various community locations. 14186 Fir Street Suite 203 Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Low-income pet owners or individuals in urgent or emergency situations. Hours: 2nd and 4th Wednesday of the month 9am-noon, by appointment only, 3rd Saturday of the month 9am-1:30pm If food is urgently needed outside of service hours, please call to coordinate sooner pick-up if available. Email (503) 655-3663 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FISH FOOD BANK FISH FOOD BANK CASCADE LOCKS FOOD PANTRIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a 3-5 day supply of emergency food. Pet food is sometimes available. Donations are accepted. 140 SW Wa Na Pa Street Cascade Locks, OR 97014 Eligibility: Low-income households Hours: 4th Wednesday of the month 2pm-6pm and Saturday following 4th Wednesday 11am-noon. Check for holiday hours in November, December, and January. Email (541) 386-3474 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FISH FOOD BANK FISH FOOD BANK HOOD RIVER FOOD PANTRIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a 3-5 day supply of emergency food. Pet food is sometimes available. Donations are accepted. 1130 Tucker Road Hood River, OR 97031 Eligibility: Low-income households Hours: Monday/Friday 3pm-5pm, Wednesday 9am-10am and 3pm-5:30pm Email (541) 386-3474 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FISH FOOD BANK FISH FOOD BANK PARKDALE FOOD PANTRIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a 3-5 day supply of emergency food. Pet food is sometimes available. Donations are accepted. 4910 Baseline Drive Mount Hood Parkdale, OR 97041 Eligibility: Low-income households Hours: 1st, 3rd, and 5th Mondays of the month 4pm-6pm; check for holiday hours in December and January Email (541) 352-3500 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FOOD FOR FAMILIES DONATIONS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accepts donations of shelf stable food items such as oils, flour, canned proteins, spices, and shelf stable milk. Also accepts donations of diapers, dog and cat food, personal and feminine hygiene products, canvas grocery bags, quart and gallon resealable plastic bags, disposable gloves, and produce bags. 3505 SE 182nd Avenue Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: No standard hours Email (971) 220-6679 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FRIENDS OF THE ANIMALS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides cat and dog services including adoption, foster and volunteer opportunities, an events calendar, and pet resources. Accepts donations from their wish list page as well as those of a monetary nature. 1875 Highway 99 N Suite B-10 Ashland, OR 975359752 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Ashland office phone: Tuesday-Friday noon-4pm Donation drop off: Monday 1pm-4pm Email (541) 708-0090 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GREENHILL HUMANE SOCIETY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides animal adoptions; reunification services; emergency boarding in crisis situations such as domestic violence, medical emergencies, disaster response, and other situations determined to be such; spay/neuter services for feral cats; a pet food pantry; foster care; humane education; dog licenses; and volunteer opportunities. 88530 Green Hill Road Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 7 days per week 11am-6pm Email (541) 689-1503 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HEARTLAND HUMANE SHELTER AND CARE HEARTLAND HUMANE SOCIETY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides open-door admission, low-cost adoptions, Trap-Neuter-Return (TNR) for feral cats, and working cat adoptions for shelter animals. Offers lost and found pet assistance, a pet food pantry, low-cost vaccine clinics, a safe housing program for pets of people in crisis, and low-cost end-of-life services for the community. 398 SW Twin Oaks Circle Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday noon-6pm, Saturday-Sunday noon-5pm Email (541) 757-9000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMPACT NW IMPACT NW DANCING TREE RESOURCE CENTER WORKER OF THE DAY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides help navigating resources in the community, information about coordinated access, and information and referral services. Assists on a first-come, first-serve basis with resource information and referral. Supplies fluctuate, but when available may provide Trimet day passes, hygiene items, water/snacks, and clothing. Referrals given for services or items of need that are not accessible through the agency. Donations of unisex clothing and household item are accepted. 10055 E Burnside Street Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Office: Monday-Thursday 9am-noon and 1pm-4pm Donations: Monday-Thursday 9am-4pm Email (503) 721-1740 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JUST COMPASSION RESOURCE CENTER DONATIONS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a donation point to drop off items such as clothing, hygiene products, pet supplies, and more. Also accepts financial donations. 12264 SW Hall Boulevard Tigard, OR 97223 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: -Small item donations (such as hygiene products and pet supplies): Drop off Monday 11am-1pm or by appointment -Large item donations (such as furniture, kitchen appliances, and large clothing donations): Drop off by appointment only Email (503) 624-4666 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PORTLAND ANIMAL WELFARE TEAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides veterinary health care clinics by appointment and access to a pet supply bank. Households can access the pet supply bank monthly without appointments or pre-qualification. Veterinary care is offered by appointments or walk-in pre-scheduled drop-in clinic dates. Dates of drop-in clinics are available on website and phone voicemail. Information can also be provided in person during regular hours. Does not offer emergency, urgent, or walk-in veterinary care outside of pre-scheduled drop-in clinic dates. At veterinary clinics, maximum of two pets per household will be seen. Services include: -Basic health exams -Vaccines -Flea and de-worming treatments -Pet supplies -Discounted animal licensing Accepts donations of new and gently used pet supplies and some medications. Check website for specific donation requirements. Cannot accept: Any food past one year of expiration, CBD treats or supplements, retractable leashes, shock collars, electric collars, or prong collars. Volunteer opportunities are available. 1718 NE 82nd Avenue Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: Pets of people who are experiencing homelessness or extreme poverty. Monthly income must be below the federal poverty level. Pets must be spayed/neutered or client must be willing to spay/neuter their pet. County Licensing: Clackamas, Multnomah, and Washington County residents Volunteers: Age 16 and older Hours: Tuesday-Thursday 10am-3pm Veterinary care: By appointment only Vaccine and Wellness Clinics: Varies Email (503) 206-6033 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PRO-BONE-O VOLUNTEERS AND DONATIONS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides volunteer opportunities at a free veterinary clinic. No experience, medical or otherwise, is required. Also accepts donations of money, pet food, kennels, crates, and other items. 1509 W 1st Avenue Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Not applicable Email (541) 799-0441 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ROGUE VALLEY STREET DOGS PET CARE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides spay and neuter services, pet gear such as collars, harnesses, and bowls, pet food, and financial assistance for urgent veterinary care when available. Does not cover vet bills already accrued. Volunteer opportunities available and accepts donations of pet food, supplies, and gear as well as financial donations. Eligibility: Low-income households or homeless individuals Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 816-6035 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAVING GRACE HUMANE SOCIETY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an animal shelter that cares for homeless, abandoned, or neglected animals. Offers a pet food pantry when supplies are available and accepts pet food donations. The shelter maintains lost-and-found pet reports for Douglas County and ensures that all adoptable cats and dogs are spayed or neutered and microchipped before adoption. Pets are accepted for surrender only in emergencies and as space allows, and low-cost spay and neuter surgeries are offered on a limited basis. 450 Old Del Rio Road Roseburg, OR 97471 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Tuesday-Saturday noon-7pm Email (541) 672-3907 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SEVERE WEATHER JACKSON COUNTY ACCESS MEDFORD SEVERE WEATHER SHELTER DONATIONS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accepting donations of blankets, pillows, and sheets; hygiene items including feminine products and adult diapers and pads; new and gently used winter clothing; and unopened pet food. 332 W 6th Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: No standard hours (458) 488-1220 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SEVERE WEATHER JACKSON COUNTY ASHLAND EMERGENCY WINTER SHELTER DONATIONS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accepts donations of clothing, personal hygiene items, shelf stable food, pet food, bedding and linen, camping gear and tents, and monetary. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Office: Monday-Friday 9am-2pm Email (541) 631-2235 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SNOWCAP COMMUNITY CHARITIES SNOWCAP COMMUNITY CHARITIES DONATIONS DONATIONS AND VOLUNTEERING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accepts donations of vehicles, commercially packaged food (canned, boxed, fresh, frozen, or dried), baby food/formula, pet food, clothing, socks, shoes, blankets, bedding, pillows, towels, washcloths, personal care/hygiene products, new toys, books, school supplies, and diapers (infant, toddler, or adult). Offers volunteer opportunities for a variety of roles. 17788 SE Pine Street Portland, OR 97233 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (503) 674-8785 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOUTH BENTON RESOURCE CENTER FOOD PANTRIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a food pantry with canned goods, fresh produce, bread, and dairy products. Distributes pet food, diapers, personal care and hygiene products when available. Offers information and referrals to agencies for various assistance. Accepts donations of shelf-stable food, condiments, personal care and hygiene products, diapers, and pet food. 648 Orchard Street Monroe, OR 97456 Eligibility: USDA income guidelines of 300% Federal Poverty Level (FPL) Hours: Tuesday 10am-noon 3rd Thursday of the month 4pm-6pm 1st, 2nd, 4th, and 5th Thursdays of the month 10am-noon Emergency: Call or email if outside of normal pantry hours Email (541) 745-8759 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GLIDE REVITALIZATION DONATIONS PROGRAM BROOKINGS CORE RESPONSE PEER HOUSE DONATIONS CASCADE AIDS PROJECT ESTHER'S PANTRY DALLAS FOOD BANK FOOD PANTRY ESTACADA AREA FOOD BANK ESTACADA AREA FOOD BANK FIDO PET FOOD BANK FIDO PET FOOD BANK FISH FOOD BANK FOOD PANTRIES FISH FOOD BANK FOOD PANTRIES FISH FOOD BANK FOOD PANTRIES FOOD FOR FAMILIES DONATIONS FRIENDS OF THE ANIMALS FRIENDS OF THE ANIMALS GREENHILL HUMANE SOCIETY GREENHILL HUMANE SOCIETY HEARTLAND HUMANE SHELTER AND CARE HEARTLAND HUMANE SOCIETY IMPACT NW WORKER OF THE DAY JUST COMPASSION RESOURCE CENTER DONATIONS PORTLAND ANIMAL WELFARE TEAM PORTLAND ANIMAL WELFARE TEAM PRO-BONE-O VOLUNTEERS AND DONATIONS SAVING GRACE HUMANE SOCIETY SAVING GRACE HUMANE SOCIETY SEVERE WEATHER JACKSON COUNTY DONATIONS SNOWCAP COMMUNITY CHARITIES DONATIONS AND VOLUNTEERING SOUTH BENTON RESOURCE CENTER FOOD PANTRIES
